title: SSRF, XML, Content, Query, Abuse, Secrets, and Retention Defense · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in threat-modeled feed-service control set and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow.
Combine URL grammar, allow or deny policy, address validation, redirect checks, proxy controls, egress isolation, metadata blocking, and rebinding tests.
Align wire, decompression, XML, text, HTML sanitation, search, pagination, bulk, and export limits so one stage cannot amplify another.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Retention Deletion
steps.instruction:
Order the Feed Go Retention Deletion Feed aggregation and Go source-to-reader-and-recovery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Retention Deletion.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Feed Go Retention Deletion assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Retention Deletion, keep Feed Go Retention Deletion connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 6482 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove defense-in-depth SSRF, parser and query budgets, secrets, retention, deletion, and residual ownership; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set purpose-bound retention for raw responses, content revisions, logs, reading state, accounts, backups, legal holds, and deletion verification.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Retention Deletion
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-retention-deletion-1le238" that uses case 3524 to prove defense-in-depth SSRF, parser and query budgets, secrets, retention, deletion, and residual ownership. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Retention Deletion.
